title: Stacked Changes, Maintainer Workflows, and Integration Queues · Guided Repository Studio
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol for stacked changes, maintainer workflows, and integration queues. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.
Use range-diff and interdiff-style evidence to explain rerolls, reordered commits, changed commit messages, and semantic deltas between series.
Compare topic-branch, integration-branch, subsystem, patch-flow, merge-queue, and release-train workflows by authority and recovery cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G21 is a workshop in a disposable repository. the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies. The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for represent dependent changes as explicit base and tip relationships, keep each review independently testable, and update downstream stacks after revision.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-collaboration-policy-defense-s2zhmf" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Collaboration Policy Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-collaboration-policy-defense-s2zhmf
# scenario-key: trace-s2zhmf probe-fmhz2s boundary-zhmfs2 owner-s2zhmfx fault-s2zhmfz
# before-ref: main=a11ces2 candidate=b00b1mf
# hypothesis: the reroll changes only the requested validation patch and preserves stack dependencies
git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2
# expected: old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible
# observation: one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent
# changed-case: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree
# after-ref: main=a11ces2 candidate=c0ffeef
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published
# transfer-limit: provider merge queues need separate event, identity, permission, and tested-revision verification
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

The learner must predict old and new series map by patch identity with reordered and amended entries visible, run git range-diff main...stack-v1 main...stack-v2, explain one patch changed, two map exactly, and the dependent tip moved to a new parent, test this changed condition: reorder the dependency after its consumer and reject the stack despite an equal final tree, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain both series refs and review comments until the integrated exact revision is published. Competency probe: Define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One universal branching model is best for every project size, release cadence, and trust structure.

stimulus.title: Gita Collaboration Policy Defense failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: One universal branching model is best for every project size, release cadence, and trust structure.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Collaboration Policy Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Gita Collaboration Policy Defense output and hide the violated stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Collaboration Policy Defense without its stated preconditions, omit the stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Collaboration Policy Defense for stacked-change and maintainer integration protocol.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define authorship, signoff, review, update, merge, rewrite, rollback, deletion, archival, and exception rules with observable enforcement.
